ab: ihan nam tamazu’an na lumaq tu uva’azng, pantu’in na mulusqu’ cam munhan Taiciu’, civung ihan baq miqumic.
ch: 小時候我們家住在地利，上學以後，我們就搬到了台中，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in tamazu’an. After I started school, we moved to Taichung and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: munhanin pantu’daing, masipul cak pali’unin iniqumican takisilazan, tu’ipin cak maqanciap icia takitaivang di vevivevi sidoq tu iniqumican.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: pangqa, matu pal’u’un haip qamisan, dusa cak ki masitoqas nak munhan acang macingav madadengaz, na’asa maqanciap anak’anak sidoq tu iniqumican.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: mavo’unin qamisan na qudas, qala talmintukang sadu’an.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: cingavan zami tu ma’aq qa qancepun cia qalinga, malmananu’ naip malasbunun, maqanciap amin malastolu cin malasput hezang malaslepun.
ch: 我們問他會哪些語言，他的布農說得很流利，也會說漢語及閩南語和日本話。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in Bunun, as well as Chinese, Hokkien and Japanese.

ab: cingavan zami naip tudipin cam maqanciap, tikicang naip tu pinanamin malaslepun, matu kukuzakin na madia pasaqalan put cin tolu, pangqa madia cia ka qancepun qalinga.
ch: 我們問他才知道，原來他小時候就學過日本話，而他工作上認識許多閩南人和漢人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, and he knows many Hokkien and Chinese at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: matu macingav cam ma, madia cia madadengaz za palcicia takiqabas qanup tu snkukuza.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: qaltu ni cam amin itqal, qala kuci’an zami lingva lasasing, namaqtu uqna’un manengkal tan’a.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: matu atikicang cam ma nitu saqal anak’anak sidoq tu iniqumican, miliskin anak’anak tu bunun na maz’avdaingaz.
ch: 小時候我們不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: malassaqalin na, miliskin anak’anak tu bunnun na, tutuza’ tu makisvevi, heza’an kainaskalan.
ch: 少為瞭解愈之以後，愈覺得自己身為布農族人，是很獨特的，也很值得高興。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a Bunun is unique and something to be proud of.
